input.user_input: Expand the indemnification clause to also cover data breaches and regulatory fines, not just third-party IP claims.

actual:
I do not currently have a document uploaded to modify. If you upload your agreement, I can directly edit the indemnification clause for you. 

In the meantime, here is a sample of how you can expand a standard IP indemnification clause to also cover data breaches and regulatory fines:

**Sample Expanded Indemnification Clause:**

"**Indemnification.** [Indemnifying Party] shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless [Indemnified Party], its affiliates, and their respective officers, directors, employees, and agents from and against any and all claims, losses, liabilities, damages, expenses, and costs (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising out of or resulting from: 
(a) any claim by a third party that the [Services/Deliverables] infringe, misappropriate, or otherwise violate such third party's intellectual property rights; 
(b) any unauthorized access, use, disclosure, alteration, or loss of [Indemnified Party]'s data, confidential information, or personal data in [Indemnifying Party]'s possession or control (a "Data Breach"); and 
(c) any fines, penalties, or sanctions imposed by a governmental or regulatory authority arising from [Indemnifying Party]'s violation of applicable laws or regulations, including but not limited to applicable data protection and privacy laws."

If you would like me to update your specific contract, please upload the file and I will make the exact edits to your existing clause.
